description:
Add an optional reviewer step before delegated merge-back, using a separately configurable reviewer model.

Scope:
- support a reviewer-agent pass over the delegated worktree commits/diff together with the bead, task goals, and relevant context
- use explicit review outcomes: approve, approve-with-nits, request-changes
- require the orchestrator to assess whether review feedback is actually valid in the context of the bead intent, task goals, and surrounding constraints before acting on it
- avoid blindly applying arbitrary or irrelevant reviewer comments that do not match the real purpose of the change
- when feedback is valid and requires fixes, run a bounded remediation pass and then re-review
- support reviewer gating in both immediate-landing and deferred-landing modes
- make the reviewer provider/model configurable independently from the main worker/orchestrator model
- surface clear operator-facing state for approved, nits-only, changes-requested, remediation-in-progress, and review-blocked cases
- add tests for review verdict handling, invalid-feedback rejection, remediation/re-review, and reviewer-model configuration

Goal: delegated landing should be able to include a realistic second-pass review without surrendering the final judgment to arbitrary reviewer comments or removing orchestrator ownership of the landing flow.
title: Add reviewer-agent gating before delegated merge-back with intent-aware review handling
